title: Not possible to pull image using IPv6 IP address
body:
# Not possible to pull image using IPv6 IP address #

/kind bug

**Description**

It is not possible to pull images from an IPv6 server by it's IP address.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull [fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo
or 
2. podman pull fd12:3456:789a:1::1:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo

**Describe the results you received:**

> Error: error pulling image "[fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo": unable to pull [fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo: error getting default registries to try: invalid reference format

or

> Error: error pulling image "fd12:3456:789a:1::1:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo": unable to pull fd12:3456:789a:1::1:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api:current-tripleo: error getting default registries to try: invalid reference format


**Describe the results you expected:**

Expect podman to connect to the registry an pull the image.

**UPDATE**
 Of the above examples we probably can only make the one with brackets around the IPv6 address work. i.e `podman pull [fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/namespace/image:tag` should work.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.5-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
There seem to be a layer of issues handling IPv6 addresses.
1. ReferenceRegexp in [1] returns an empty array.
2. When I try to statically override the regular expression parsing with the below code it looks like the host/port part is stripped from the request URL:

```
189 func Parse(s string) (Reference, error) {
190         // matches := ReferenceRegexp.FindStringSubmatch(s)
191         matches := []string{}
192         matches = append(matches, "[fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api")
193         matches = append(matches, "[fd12:3456:789a:1::1]:8787/tripleomaster/centos-binary-ironic-api")
194         matches = append(matches, "current-tripleo")
195         matches = append(matches, "")
```

[1] https://github.com/containers/image/blob/master/docker/reference/regexp.go#L68-L70
Hm. Seems like c/image - could you file an issue against them as well? https://github.com/containers/image

Hi @hjensas, thanks for opening the issue.  The problem is that it's an invalid docker reference and can't be supported without changing pretty much every Docker-reference parser in the wild. Docker doesn't support it either.

A workaround is adding an entry for ` fd12:3456:789a:1::1:` to `/etc/hosts` and use the DNS entry for referencing images on the registry.
I am closing the issue (as we can't fix it) but we can keep the discussion going.

@mheon agreed, there is an issue in container/image. This issue with a proposed fix seems to be related: https://github.com/docker/distribution/pull/2899, however the fix does'nt seem to work.

@vrothberg, It's absurd that software built after 2010 does'nt support ipv6 ...

Afaict the reference spec is something like these:
* Without host: `[PATH/]IMAGE_NAME[:TAG]`
* With host: `ADDRESS[:PORT][/PATH]/IMAGE_NAME[:TAG]`.

**Is the docker reference format is un-parsable?**
If [PATH/] can have multiple levels there is no way to determine the difference between `path0/path1/image:tag` and `host-shortname/path/image:tag`?

If this is the case it's about time the container community start introducing a way to reference images. May a suggest using a well established standard such as the Uniform Resource Identifier[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniform_Resource_Identifier#Generic_syntax) (URI) schema.

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Uniform_Resource_Identifier#Generic_syntax

> @vrothberg, It's absurd that software built after 2010 does'nt support ipv6 ...

I understand the frustration but note that "not supporting ipv6" isn't entirely accurate. The Docker-reference format just doesn't allow to encode ipv6 addresses. Using DNS still allows for communication with the registry.

> **Is the docker reference format is un-parsable?**
> If [PATH/] can have multiple levels there is no way to determine the difference between `path0/path1/image:tag` and `host-shortname/path/image:tag`?

There are countless corner cases, the heavy use of unqualified images (e.g., `alpine:latest` vs `docker.io/library/alpine:latest`), special treatment of `localhost` but only by _some_ tools, implicitly adding `:latest` if no tag is specified, etc.

Pretty much all parsers rely on `docker/distribution` and that's where it must be addressed. The `containers/image` library can't diverge as the risk of introducing regressions is high.
